canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, COCO is consistently defined and used as a benchmark to evaluate image-to-text description generation. Its most prevalent application, supported by extensive evidence, is to measure model performance on the `Image captioning` task. Papers frequently cite its use for the "COCO captioning task" and position it as a standard instrument for this purpose. A more specialized use is also documented, where its validation set is employed to measure `Object hallucination` within visual captions. While its primary role centers on text generation from images, COCO is also reported as a measurement instrument for a wider array of behaviors, including `Semantic segmentation`, `Object detection`, `Pose estimation`, `Image generation`, `Cross-modal retrieval`, and `Multimodal alignment`, although the provided data lacks specific evidence detailing these applications.
